name: Rule of Thirds
description: Places key subjects along imaginary grid lines dividing the image into thirds, creating a balanced feel.

name: Center Symmetrical (Kubrick style)
description: Aligns the main subject directly in the center with symmetrical framing, creating intense, powerful shots.

name: Dutch Angle (Tilted)
description: Tilts the camera slightly off-center to create tension, psychological unease, or dramatic energy.

name: Extreme Close-Up (ECU)
description: Focuses tightly on a specific detail, such as eyes or a hand, amplifying emotion and intimacy.

name: Wide Establishing Shot
description: Highlights the subject within a massive landscape or city, providing scale and environmental context.

name: Low-Angle Hero Shot
description: Shoots upwards at the subject from a low height, making them appear powerful, majestic, or intimidating.

name: Leading Lines Perspective
description: Uses lines in the environment (e.g. roads, walls, tracks) to guide the viewer's eyes directly to the subject.

name: Golden Spiral / Fibonacci Framing
description: Arranges elements along a spiral path to naturally guide focus with beautiful, organic flow.
